note: Human-curated seed of external chemical knowledge-graph / linked-data sources for ChemField. tools/build_kg_sources.py compiles this into data/chemfield-kg-sources.json, computing a stable cid/ual per source and linking every source into an OriginTrail-prepared asset. Volatile metrics (live counts, versions) are NOT stored here; they are refreshed into snapshotStats by tools/refresh_kg_sources.py. 'distribution' governs whether ChemField may redistribute derived data: 'redistributable' (open, attribution kept) vs 'reference_only' (commercial/closed/share-alike — link and cite, do not vendor).

sources.title: Materials Project
sources.licenseExceptions: GNoME-derived subset is CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial) and MUST be excluded from ChemField redistribution.
sources.chemfieldUse: Primary turnkey machine-accessible source for V/Cr redox speciation via MPRester.get_pourbaix_entries(); build node-per-species field/1 graphs.
sources.note: pymatgen + mp-api. Cite Materials Project per CC-BY.

sources.title: OPTIMADE federation
sources.licenseExceptions: Each federated provider carries its own licence; verify per provider before redistribution.
sources.chemfieldUse: Single uniform REST entrypoint across AFLOW/OQMD/COD/JARVIS/NOMAD/Materials Cloud/Alexandria for inorganic structures.
sources.note: 26M+ structures across ~19 available providers; counts refreshed into snapshotStats.

sources.title: MatKG / MatKG-2
sources.chemfieldUse: Filter triples on vanadium/chromium/slag/leaching to seed thematic 3D force-graph subgraphs. RDF/CSV -> field/1 JSON.
sources.note: 70k entities / 5.4M triples. DOI 10.1038/s41597-024-03039-z. Links to Wikidata/DBpedia/Materials Project.

sources.title: Crystallography Open Database (COD)
sources.chemfieldUse: CC0 mineral/oxide structures for ore & slag phases; 3D structure via Mol*. Also reachable through OPTIMADE.

sources.title: Wikidata
sources.chemfieldUse: SPARQL -> JSON backbone skeleton of elements/minerals/metals; owl:sameAs targets for field/1 nodes.

sources.title: PubChemRDF
sources.chemfieldUse: InChIKey <-> CID resolution and sameAs links; ingest only relevant inorganic/aqueous-ion subsets (dataset is huge, ~80B triples).
sources.note: US-Gov public domain. No official own SPARQL endpoint (use IDSM/ChemWebRDF).

sources.title: ChEBI
sources.chemfieldUse: Typing/classification overlay for species; ontology graph loads directly into force-graph.

sources.title: RRUFF / Raman Open Database
sources.chemfieldUse: High-quality mineral records with spectra & cell parameters for ore/slag mineralogy; ROD shares the COD model.

sources.title: The World Avatar (J-Park Simulator)
sources.chemfieldUse: Architecture inspiration for ChemField's field/1 layer (cross-domain ontologies + agents + content-linking); reference ontology patterns, do not vendor.
sources.note: Most ambitious chemistry-KG initiative; verify OntoSpecies counts at the source.

sources.title: EMMO + domain-electrochemistry + MSEO
sources.licenseExceptions: MSEO is MIT-licensed; EMMO core is CC-BY-4.0.
sources.chemfieldUse: Semantic backbone to type field/1 terms consistently; EMMO v1.0.0 (Feb 2025) + domain-electrochemistry.
sources.note: OWL/RDF. Reusable building blocks: OntoTrans, MarketPlace, OYSTER, CHAMEO.

sources.title: PHREEQC thermodynamic databases
sources.chemfieldUse: Wrap .dat databases (llnl/minteq/wateq4f/pitzer/sit) as field/1 nodes: species, phases, reactions, log K. Tabular -> graph. Primary leaching-speciation source.
sources.note: Public domain (USGS). R-package phreeqc. Activity models differ per DB.

sources.title: GEMS / GEM-Selektor (PSI)
sources.licenseExceptions: GEMS3K solver is GPL-3.0: invoke as a separate process; do NOT link into Apache-2.0 code. Thermodata (PSI-Nagra TDB, Cemdata) carry their own licences.
sources.chemfieldUse: Slag & leaching equilibrium thermodynamics; call GEMS3K/xGEMS out-of-process to avoid GPL contamination of Apache code.
sources.note: GUI GPL-3.0 since v3.6.0. Python via xGEMS.

sources.title: USGS Mineral Resources Online Spatial Data (MRDATA)
sources.chemfieldUse: Build ore/deposit subgraphs (e.g. vanadium-bearing deposits) linking geology to composition; US-gov public domain.
sources.note: Mostly geospatial/tabular; construct graph from records.

sources.title: Hetionet
sources.chemfieldUse: Reference exemplar for KG build & Neo4j->3D pipeline; low direct hydrometallurgy relevance.

sources.title: FactSage / FACT (FToxid slag data)
sources.chemfieldUse: Highest-quality slag thermochemistry BUT proprietary: cite and link only; never vendor or redistribute derived data.

sources.title: Inorganic Crystal Structure Database (ICSD)
sources.chemfieldUse: Most complete inorganic structures BUT licence excludes open reuse: reference only; prefer COD/OPTIMADE for redistributable data.

visualizationStack.title: 3d-force-graph (Vasturiano)
visualizationStack.note: MVP start; ngraph engine for large graphs; VR/AR bindings.

visualizationStack.title: PyKEEN + Embedding Projector
visualizationStack.note: KG embeddings (RotatE) -> UMAP/t-SNE 3D for V/Cr similarity.
